DISCOVERY 525, DEFENDANT MUNKHTUR NYAMOCHIR'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF; INCLUDING TRAVEL TO SAN FRANCISCO AT DEFENDANT'S EXPENSE IS GRANTED. DEFENSE SHALL PAY REASONABLE EXPENSES FOR PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND MENTAL EXAMINATION. TICKETS FOR THE FLIGHT TO AND FROM SAN FRANCISCO SHALL OFFER NO WORSE ACCOMMODATIONS THAN PLAINTIFF USED IN HIS PREVIOUS FLIGHTS TO SAN FRANCISCO (E.G. EXTRA LEGROOM). THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLAINCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff; Including Travel To San Francisco At Defendant'S Expense
Off Calendar Oct-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
CONOR M. KELLY, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA, KELLY & SCHOENBERGER (415) 981-7210 ckelly@walkuplawoffice.com ATTORNEY FOR PLAINTIFF
IAN A. SCHAEFFER, Esq., MCNAMARA, AMBACHER,WHEELER, HIRSIG &GRAY LLP (707) 427-3998
ian.schaeffer@mcnamaralaw.com Attorney for Defendant MUNKHTUR NYAMOCHIR
Adriana Valdez, Esq., WOOD, SMITH, HENNING & BERMAN LLP 925 222 3400 Attorney for Defendants UBER TECHNOLOGIES et al

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MUNKHTUR NYAMOCHIR'S Motion To Compel Independent Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff; Including Travel To San Francisco At Defendant'S Expense

Motion granted. Defense shall pay reasonable expenses for Plaintiff to attend mental examination. Tickets for the flight to and from San Francisco shall offer no worse accommodations than Plaintiff used in his previous flights to San Francisco (e.g. extra legroom).

Cousel for Defendant MUNKHTUR NYAMOCHIRis required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and is to include additional languge as discussed during today's hearing, in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
